grade_description: Judicial Salary Group 1 — Lady Chief Justice of England and Wales. Single-holder post at the head of the Judiciary of England and Wales.
Judicial Salary Group 1.1 — President of the UK Supreme Court, Lord President of the Court of Session (Scotland), Lady Chief Justice of Northern Ireland, Master of the Rolls.
Judicial Salary Group 2 — Justices of the Supreme Court; Division Presidents (Family/King's Bench); Chancellor of the High Court; Senior President of Tribunals; Lord Justice Clerk (Scotland).
Judicial Salary Group 3 — Lords/Lady Justices of Appeal (England & Wales and Northern Ireland); Inner House Judges of the Court of Session (Scotland).

Judicial Salary Group 4 — Puisne Judges of the High Court (England & Wales and Northern Ireland); Outer House Judges of the Court of Session (Scotland); Vice-Chancellor of the County Palatine of Lancaster.

Judicial Salary Group 5 — Senior Circuit Judges; Chamber Presidents of First-tier Tribunals; Judge Advocate General; President of Employment Tribunals; Sheriffs Principal (Scotland); Senior District Judge (Chief Magistrate); Recorder of Liverpool/Manchester/Belfast.
Judicial Salary Group 5.1 — Senior Masters & Registrars (England & Wales); Upper Tribunal Judges; Social Security Commissioner (Northern Ireland); Deputy Chamber Presidents.

Judicial Salary Group 5.2 — Circuit Judges; Sheriffs (Scotland); Senior Judge of the Court of Protection; Regional Employment Judges; Senior Court Masters and Cost Judges; County Court Judges (Northern Ireland).
Judicial Salary Group 6 — Designated First-tier Tribunal Judges; Vice-Judge Advocate General; Regional Judges (Property Chamber); Presiding District Judge (Magistrates' Courts) Northern Ireland.

Judicial Salary Group 7 — District Judges (England & Wales, Northern Ireland); Employment Judges; Coroners (Northern Ireland); Assistant Judge Advocate General; First-tier Tribunal Judges; Summary Sheriffs (Scotland).
Judicial Salary Group 8 — Salaried (Regional) Medical Members of the First-tier Tribunal Social Entitlement Chamber.

Cabinet Minister (Secretary of State). Pay = MP + Cabinet Minister additional (£71,090). Note: Cabinet Ministers often forgo part of the additional salary by convention, so actual paid amount is typically around £159,000.
Government Chief Whip (also Parliamentary Secretary to the Treasury). Pay = MP + Chief Whip additional (£41,685).
Lord Chancellor / Secretary of State for Justice. Pay = MP + Lord Chancellor additional (£61,651).
Lord Speaker of the House of Lords, per Constitutional Reform Act 2005.
Leader of the House of Lords. Salary-only (Lords don't receive MP sessional indemnity — peers receive daily allowance).
Minister of State (senior junior minister). Pay = MP + Minister of State additional (£31,680).
Member of Parliament — sessional indemnity only (excluding any office-holder additional). Set annually by IPSA. £93,904 for 2025-26 (uplifted 5.5% from £91,346 in April 2025).
Leader of the Official Opposition. Pay = MP + Leader additional (£76,888).
Prime Minister of the United Kingdom. Pay = MP sessional indemnity (£93,904) + PM additional salary (£83,370) per Ministerial and other Salaries Act 1975.
Speaker of the House of Commons. Special statutory salary set by Ministerial and other Salaries Act 1975 s.1(1)(b).
Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State (PUSS). Pay = MP + PUSS additional (£23,712).
Permanent Secretary — Permanent Head of a ministerial department. Tier set centrally by the Cabinet Secretary; pay above the published ceiling requires Chief Secretary to the Treasury approval.
Permanent Secretary — Permanent Head of a ministerial department. Tier set centrally by the Cabinet Secretary; pay above the published ceiling requires Chief Secretary to the Treasury approval.

SCS Band 2 — Director. Heads a directorate within a department or an executive agency of moderate size.

SCS Band 3 — Director General. Heads a large directorate-general or leads a substantial group across a major department.
